title: BlindSpotCheck: IEC 62443 multi-tenant monitoring blind-spot evaluator
description:
BlindSpotCheck operationalises the SC-1 ∧ NC-1 ∧ NC-2 biconditional from W. Kim (2026) "Compliant Yet Blind: Measuring and Closing Multi-Tenant Monitoring Gaps in IEC 62443" and classifies every conduit in a multi-tenant IEC 62443 architecture as blind-spot, borderline, resolved-by-SP, or no-cross-AO.

SC-1 uses the two-disjunct form of paper §4 (endpoint asset-owner difference OR a third asset owner's artefact transiting the conduit). Transit artefact ownership follows the four-context rule of paper §3: IATA RP 1797 Application Provider for passenger session data, IEC 62443-2-4 commissioning asset owner for SP-supplied software, OpenID Connect Core 1.0 / RFC 6749 issuer for federated identity, and originating AO for intra-tenant operational traffic. NC-1 derives from IEC 62443-2-4 SP sub-type Clauses 3.1.12 and 3.1.13; NC-2 from IEC 62443-3-2 ZCR 3 Clause 4.4.

The release ships a Python CLI (authoritative evaluator), a line-for-line JavaScript port running in a browser canvas, the schema-locked YAML architecture format (`architecture-v1`), and bundled templates for airport CUPPS 1.0 (measured, reproducing paper Table 3 and Appendix D.1 per-row SC-1 trace), rail passenger station, maritime container terminal, and power-grid TSO/DSO (paper §8.1 analytic hypothesis). Cross-language parity tests lock the evaluator, the YAML round-trip, and the Appendix D.1 trace against the paper.

Reproduction: `bash reproduce-table3.sh` from a fresh clone.
